sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, Alexis, presents with numbness and tingling in her right hand, which has been ongoing for approximately six months, with increased persistence over the last month.
The numbness and tingling are present all the time, and she experiences some pain, particularly when typing on a keyboard or using a mouse.
The pain is described as some pain, and rest, ibuprofen, and avoiding typing on the keyboard or using a mouse help alleviate it.
The patient denies waking up at night due to pain or tingling.
Past medical history: [NOT MENTIONED].
Review of systems: [NOT MENTIONED].
Current medications: I prescribed ibuprofen for pain.
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED].
Physical exam: The patient has pain on extension and flexion of the wrist, and a positive Tinel's sign, indicating nerve compression.
The patient has a reduced grip strength on the right hand, particularly in the thumb, index, and middle fingers, which are also experiencing numbness.
Test Results: [NOT MENTIONED].
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has carpal tunnel syndrome, likely due to repetitive strain from typing and mouse use.
Problem list: Carpal tunnel syndrome; [NOT MENTIONED].
Plan:
I would like to put the patient in a wrist splint to wear at night, and increase her ibuprofen dosage to 600mg four times a day.
I will order an EMG nerve conduction study to confirm the diagnosis.
If the test is positive, we will consider conservative treatment, but may need to consider surgery to release the impingement on the nerves.
Follow-up: I will review the results of the EMG nerve conduction study and decide on further treatment, which may include physical therapy or surgery.
